title: Secondary sex ratio among women exposed to diethylstilbestrol in utero.
summary: Study investigating the impact of in utero diethylstilbestrol (DES) exposure on the secondary sex ratio in offspring.
key_findings: DES exposure in utero may exert long-term effects on female endocrine function.
Previous studies on other chemicals (PCBs, mercury, dioxin, lead, DDT) show mixed or null results regarding secondary sex ratio.
captured_features: hormone levels (FSH, LH, progesterone, testosterone, estrogen)
pathologies_diseases: anatomic abnormalities of the reproductive tract
study_design: Not explicitly stated in provided snippet (Background section)
population: Women exposed to DES in utero
limitations: Dose and timing of exposure in many studies are incompletely characterized
